name: Lieutenant Gerald Farrow
role: Adjutant, 1st Battalion, 5th Field Artillery — primary culprit
publicFace: A slight, pale young officer from a Boston banking family, impeccably turned out even in a forward dugout. He is helpful, precise, and speaks French well — qualities that made him invaluable as a liaison conduit and, it turns out, made him useful to other parties.
privateGoal: To pass the corrected fire tables — which he has altered to create a 200-meter gap in coverage over the eastern approach to Cantigny — to the German artillery liaison via Captain Renard's network, allowing a German counter-attack to exploit the unprotected corridor.
fear: Being caught before the German counter-attack validates his work — if he is exposed now, his entire network is burned and he has accomplished nothing
knowledge: He knows the exact grid reference of the gap he created in the barrage coverage
He knows Captain Renard is a French officer working for German military intelligence and has been his handler for four months
He cut the forward wire himself at 04:15, before the Signaler's party went forward, using a pair of German-issue wire cutters he obtained through Renard
voice: Measured, helpful, slightly over-precise. Uses technical vocabulary to create the impression of competence and to slow down conversations that are getting close to dangerous territory.
trustLogic: Responds warmly to anyone who seems to accept his explanations at face value. Becomes subtly evasive when pressed on timeline specifics — his alibi has a 45-minute gap he cannot account for.
secrets: He has been passing intelligence to German military networks through Captain Renard since January 1918
He carries a second set of wire cutters — German-manufactured Feldzange — in the lining of his map case

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Produces a plausible alternative explanation with specific-sounding details designed to redirect suspicion toward Sergeant Voss
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Becomes very still. Stops using technical vocabulary. Asks to see the evidence directly — he is assessing whether it is sufficient to convict
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: The German wire cutters in his map case — he will never voluntarily produce them
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the key evidence clue clue_cut_wire_splice is presented to him directly in the presence of a witness
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Walks calmly toward the communication trench as if on an errand, then sprints for the eastern rubble line during a smoke barrage
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Uses his knowledge of the trench layout to take indirect routes; will discard his map case if pursued
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Invokes his family's political connections and demands to speak to a senior JAG officer; denies everything with practiced calm

represents: Lieutenants of the 28th Infantry Regiment who were present during the artillery coordination phase at Cantigny on May 28, 1918
